Finally, this juxtaposition of the verb вАЬto cutвАЭ and вАЬcovenantвАЭ normally is followed by the preposition вАЬwith,вАЭ while here it is вАЬfrom.вАЭ The translation above assumes an emendation of вАП„Х÷Ј„™÷Љ÷і„Ы÷∞„®÷Є„™вАО (vatikhrat, вАЬand you cutвАЭ) to вАП„Х÷∞„Ы÷Є„®÷і„Щ„™вАО (vekharit, вАЬand you purchaseвАЭ) from the root вАП„Ы÷Љ÷Є„®÷Є„ФвАО (kharah); see HALOT 497 s.v. II вАП„Ы„®„ФвАО.
n2305723: tn The Hebrew text has simply вАП„Ч÷Є„Ц÷Є„ФвАО (khazah, вАЬgazeвАЭ). The adverb вАЬlonginglyвАЭ is interpretive (see the context, where sexual lust is depicted).
n2305724: tn Heb вАЬ[at] a hand you gaze.вАЭ The term вАП„Щ÷Є„УвАО (yad, вАЬhandвАЭ) probably has the sense of вАЬpower, manhoodвАЭ here, where it is used, as in Ugaritic, as a euphemism for the genitals. See HALOT 387 s.v. I вАП„Щ÷Є„УвАО.
n2305726: tn Heb вАЬthe king.вАЭ Since the context refers to idolatry and child sacrifice (see v. 5), some emend вАП„Ю÷ґ„Ь÷ґ„ЪвАО (melekh, вАЬkingвАЭ) to вАЬMolech.вАЭ Perhaps IsraelвАЩs devotion to her idols is likened here to a subject taking tribute to a ruler.
n2305728: sn IsraelвАЩs devotion to her idols is inordinate, irrational, and self-destructive.
n2305729: tn Heb вАЬby the greatness [i.e., вАЬlength,вАЭ see BDB 914 s.v. вАП„®÷є„СвАО 2] of your way you get tired.вАЭ
n2305730: tn Heb вАЬit is hopelessвАЭ (so NAB, NASB, NIV); NRSV вАЬIt is useless.вАЭ
n2305731: tn Heb вАЬthe life of your hand you find.вАЭ The term вАП„Ч÷Ј„Щ÷Љ÷Є„ФвАО (khayyah, вАЬlifeвАЭ) is here used in the sense of вАЬrenewalвАЭ (see BDB 312 s.v.) while вАП„Щ÷Є„УвАО (yad) is used of вАЬstrength.вАЭ
n2305733: tn Heb вАЬyou do not place [it] on your heart.вАЭ
n2305734: tn Heb вАЬIs it not [because] I have been silent, and from long ago?вАЭ
n2305735: sn GodвАЩs patience with sinful Israel has caused them to think that they can sin with impunity and suffer no consequences.
n2305736: tn Heb вАЬI, I will declare your righteousness and your deeds.вАЭ
n2305737: tn The Hebrew text has вАП„І÷і„С÷Љ„Х÷Љ„¶÷Ј„Щ÷і„Ъ÷∞вАО (qibbutsayikh, вАЬyour gatheringsвАЭ), an otherwise unattested noun from the verbal root вАП„І÷Є„С÷Ј„•вАО (qavats, вАЬgatherвАЭ). Perhaps this alludes to their religious assemblies and by metonymy to their rituals. Since idolatry is a prominent theme in the context, some understand this as a reference to a collection of idols. The second half of the verse also favors this view.
n2305738: tn Heb вАЬall of them a wind lifts up.вАЭ
n2305740: tn Or вАЬseeks refuge in me.вАЭ вАЬSeeking refugeвАЭ is a metonymy for вАЬbeing loyal to.вАЭ
n2305741: tn Heb вАЬpossess, own.вАЭ The point seems to be that he will have free access to GodвАЩs presence, as if GodвАЩs temple mount were his personal possession.
n2305742: tn Since God is speaking throughout this context, perhaps we should emend the text to вАЬand I say.вАЭ However, divine speech is introduced in v. 15.
n2305743: tn Heb вАЬthe one who dwells forever.вАЭ вАП„©„Б÷є„Ы÷µ„Я „Ґ÷Ј„УвАО (shokhen  њad) is sometimes translated вАЬthe one who lives forever,вАЭ and understood as a reference to GodвАЩs eternal existence. However, the immediately preceding and following descriptions (вАЬhigh and exaltedвАЭ and вАЬholyвАЭ) emphasize his sovereign rule. In the next line, he declares, вАЬI dwell in an exalted and holy [place],вАЭ which refers to the place from which he rules. Therefore it is more likely that вАП„©„Б÷є„Ы÷µ„Я „Ґ÷Ј„УвАО (shokhen  њad) means вАЬI dwell [in my lofty palace] foreverвАЭ and refers to GodвАЩs eternal kingship.
n2305744: tn Heb вАЬand also with the crushed and lowly of spirit.вАЭ This may refer to the repentant who have humbled themselves (see 66:2) or more generally to the exiles who have experienced discouragement and humiliation.
n2305745: tn Heb вАЬto restore the lowly of spirit and to restore the heart of the crushed.вАЭ
n2305746: tn Or perhaps, вАЬargue,вАЭ or вАЬaccuseвАЭ (so NAB, NIV, NRSV).
n2305747: tn Heb вАЬfor a spirit from before me would be faint.вАЭ
n2305748: tn Heb вАЬand I struck him, hiding, and I was angry.вАЭ вАП„§÷Љ÷Є„†÷і„Щ„ЭвАО (panim, вАЬfaceвАЭ) is the implied object of вАЬhiding.вАЭ
n2305749: tn Heb вАЬand he walked [as an] apostate in the way of his heart.вАЭ
n2305750: tn Heb вАЬhis waysвАЭ (so KJV, NASB, NIV); TEV вАЬhow they acted.вАЭ
n2305751: tc The MT has вАП„Х÷∞„Р÷Ј„†÷∞„Ч÷µ„Ф„Х÷ЉвАО (ve Њankhehu) from вАП„†÷Є„Ч÷Є„ФвАО (nakhah) вАЬI will lead them,вАЭ but the consonantal text may also be read as вАП„Х÷Ј„Р÷≤„†÷і„Ч÷µ„Ф„Х÷ЉвАО (va Њanikhehu) from вАП„†„Х÷Љ„Ч÷ЈвАО (nuakh) вАЬI will give them rest.вАЭ The MT is supported by Aquila and the Vulgate, though 1QIsaa omits the verb and the LXX and Targum offer mixed evidence.
n2305752: tn The verb вАП„Х÷Ј„Р÷≤„©„Б÷Ј„Ь÷Љ÷µ„ЭвАО (va Њashallem), the Piel form of the verb вАП„©„Б÷Є„Ь÷µ„ЭвАО (shalem), means вАЬto make whole, make restitution, compensate, rewardвАЭ (HALOT 1534, s.v.). The noun вАП„†÷і„Ч÷ї„Ю÷і„Щ„ЭвАО (nikhumim) uses the plural form for the abstract concept, вАЬcomfort.вАЭ The Lord will bestow comfort as restitution to Israel.
n2305753: tn Heb вАЬto him and to his mourners.вАЭ Since Israel is represented by the singular pronoun вАЬto himвАЭ (rendered as plural вАЬthemвАЭ for style throughout vv. 17-19), those who mourn for, or with, him are likely religious converts or others who sympathize with Israel (see J. D. W. Watts, Isaiah [WBC], 25:835).
n2305754: tc The Hebrew text has literally, вАЬone who creates fruit of lips.вАЭ Perhaps the pronoun вАП„Р÷≤„†÷і„ЩвАО ( Њani) should be inserted after the participle; it may have been accidentally omitted by haplography: вАП„†„Х÷Љ„С „©„В÷∞„§÷Є„™÷Є„Щ÷і„ЭвАО [вАП„Р÷≤„†÷і„ЩвАО] вАП„С÷Љ„Х÷є„®÷µ„ЩвАО (bore Њ [ Њani] nuv sefatayim). вАЬFruit of the lipsвАЭ is often understood as a metonymy for praise; perhaps it refers more generally to joyful shouts (see v. 18).
n2305755: tn Heb вАЬPeace, peace.вАЭ The repetition of the noun emphasizes degree.
n230581: tn Heb вАЬdeclare to my people their rebellion.вАЭ
n230582: tn Heb вАЬand to the house of Jacob their sin.вАЭ The verb вАЬdeclareвАЭ is understood by ellipsis (note the preceding line).
n230583: tn Heb вАЬwaysвАЭ (so KJV, NAB, NASB, NIV, NRSV, TEV); NLT вАЬmy laws.вАЭ
n230584: tn The words вАЬthey lamentвАЭ are supplied in the translation for clarification.
n230585: tn Heb вАЬyou find pleasureвАЭ; NASB вАЬyou find your desire.вАЭ
n230586: tn Or perhaps, вАЬdebtors.вАЭ See HALOT 865 s.v. * вАП„Ґ÷Є„¶÷µ„СвАО.
n230587: tn Heb вАЬyou fast forвАЭ (so NASB); NRSV вАЬyou fast only to quarrel.вАЭ
n230588: tn Heb вАЬand for striking with a sinful fist.вАЭ
n2305810: tn Heb вАЬa day when man humbles himself.вАЭ The words вАЬDo I wantвАЭ are supplied in the translation for stylistic reasons.
n2305812: tn Heb вАЬIs this not a fast I choose?вАЭ вАЬNoвАЭ is supplied in the translation for clarification.
n2305813: tn The words вАЬI want youвАЭ are supplied in the translation for stylistic reasons.
n2305815: tn Heb вАЬIs it not?вАЭ The rhetorical question here expects a positive answer, вАЬIt is!вАЭ
n2305816: tn Heb вАЬand afflicted [ones], homeless [ones] you should bring [into] a house.вАЭ On the meaning of вАП„Ю÷∞„®„Х÷Љ„У÷і„Щ„ЭвАО (merudim, вАЬhomelessвАЭ) see HALOT 633 s.v. *вАП„Ю÷Є„®„Х÷Љ„УвАО.
n2305817: tn Heb вАЬand from your flesh do not hide yourself.вАЭ
n2305818:
tn Heb вАЬwill burst out like the dawn.вАЭ
sn Light here symbolizes GodвАЩs favor and restored blessing, as the immediately following context makes clear.
n2305820: tn Or вАЬrighteousness.вАЭ Their godly behavior will be on display for all to see.
n2305821: sn The nation will experience GodвАЩs protective presence.
n2305822: tn Heb вАЬif you.вАЭ In the Hebrew text vv. 9b-10 are one long conditional sentence. The protasis (multiple вАЬifвАЭ clauses here) appears in vv. 9b-10a, with the apodosis (вАЬthenвАЭ clause) appearing in v. 10b.
n2305823: tn Heb вАЬif you.вАЭ See the note on вАЬyou mustвАЭ in v. 9b.
n2305824: tn Heb вАЬIf you furnish for the hungry [with] your being, and the appetite of the oppressed you satisfy.вАЭ
n2305826: tn Heb вАЬand your darkness [will be] like noonday.вАЭ
n2305827: tn Heb вАЬhe will satisfy in parched regions your appetite.вАЭ
n2305828: tn Heb вАЬand your bones he will strengthen.вАЭ
n2305829: tn Heb вАЬand they will build from you ancient ruins.вАЭ
n2305830: tc The Hebrew text has вАЬthe one who restores paths for dwelling.вАЭ The idea of вАЬpaths to dwell inвАЭ is not a common notion. Some have proposed emending вАП„†÷∞„™÷і„Щ„С„Х÷є„™вАО (netivot, вАЬpathsвАЭ) to вАП„†÷∞„™÷і„Щ„¶„Х÷є„™вАО (netitsot, вАЬruinsвАЭ), a passive participle from вАП„†÷Є„™÷Ј„•вАО (natats, вАЬtear downвАЭ; see HALOT 732 s.v. *вАП„†÷∞„™÷і„Щ„¶÷Є„ФвАО), because tighter parallelism with the preceding line is achieved. However, none of the textual sources support this emendation. The line may mean that paths must be repaired in order to dwell in the land.
n2305831: tn Lit., вАЬif you.вАЭ In the Hebrew text vv. 13-14 are one long conditional sentence. The protasis (multiple вАЬifвАЭ clauses here) appears in v. 13, with the apodosis (вАЬthenвАЭ clause) appearing in v. 14.
n2305832: tn Heb вАЬif you turn from the Sabbath your feet.вАЭ
n2305833: tn Heb вАЬ[from] doing your desires on my holy day.вАЭ The Qumran scroll 1QIsaa supplies the preposition вАП„Ю÷і„ЯвАО (min) on вАЬdoing.вАЭ
n2305834: tn Heb вАЬand call the Sabbath a pleasureвАЭ; KJV, NAB, NASB, NIV, NRSV вАЬa delight.вАЭ
n2305835: tn Heb вАЬand [call] the holy [day] of the Lord honored.вАЭ On вАП„І÷Є„У„Х÷є„©„БвАО (qadosh, вАЬholyвАЭ) as indicating a time period, see BDB 872 s.v. 2.e (cf. also Neh 8:9-11).
n2305836: tn Heb вАЬand you honor it [by refraining] from accomplishing your ways, from finding your desire and speaking a word.вАЭ It is unlikely that the last phrase (вАЬspeaking a wordвАЭ) is a prohibition against talking on the Sabbath; instead it probably refers to making transactions or plans (see Hos 10:4). Some see here a reference to idle talk (cf. 2 Sam 19:29).
n2305837: tn For a parallel use of the phrase вАЬfind joy inвАЭ (Hitpael of вАП„Ґ÷Є„†÷Ј„ТвАО [ њanag] followed by the preposition вАП„Ґ÷Ј„ЬвАО [ њal]), see Ps 37:4.
n2305838: tn Heb вАЬand I will cause you to ride upon the heights of the land.вАЭ The statement seems to be an allusion to Deut 32:13, where it is associated, as here, with GodвАЩs abundant provision of food.
n2305839: tn Heb вАЬand I will cause you to eat the inheritance of Jacob your father.вАЭ The Hebrew term вАП„†÷Ј„Ч÷≤„Ь÷Є„ФвАО (nakhalah) likely stands by metonymy for the crops that grow on JacobвАЩs вАЬinheritanceвАЭ (i.e., the land he inherited as a result of GodвАЩs promise).
n2305840: tn Heb вАЬfor the mouth of the Lord has spoken.вАЭ The introductory вАП„Ы÷Љ÷і„ЩвАО (ki) may be asseverative (as reflected in the translation) or causal/explanatory, explaining why the preceding promise will become reality (because it is guaranteed by the divine word).
n230592: tn Heb вАЬor his ear too heavy [i.e., вАЬdullвАЭ] to hear.вАЭ
n230593: tn Heb вАЬand your sins have caused [his] face to be hidden from you so as not to hear.вАЭ
n230597: tn Heb вАЬthat which is pressed in hatches [as] a snake.вАЭ
n230598: tn Heb вАЬtheir deeds are deeds of sin, and the work of violence [is] in their hands.вАЭ
n2305910: tn Heb вАЬthey quickly pour out innocent blood.вАЭ
n2305911: tn Heb вАЬtheir thoughts are thoughts of sin, destruction and crushing [are] in their roadways.вАЭ
n2305912: tn Heb вАЬa way of peace they do not know, and there is no justice in their pathways.вАЭ
n2305913: tn Heb вАЬtheir paths they make crooked; everyone who walks in it does not know peace.вАЭ
n2305914: tn вАП„Ю÷і„©„Б÷∞„§÷Љ÷Є„ШвАО (mishpat), which refers to вАЬjusticeвАЭ in the earlier verses, here refers to вАЬjustice from God,вАЭ or вАЬvindication.вАЭ Because the people are unjust, God refuses to vindicate them before their enemies. See v. 11.
n2305915: sn The prophet speaks on behalf of the sinful nation and confesses its sins.
n2305916: sn Light here symbolizes prosperity and blessing.
n2305917: tn Heb вАЬbut, look, darknessвАЭ; NIV вАЬbut all is darkness.вАЭ
n2305918: tn The words вАЬwe wait forвАЭ are supplied in the translation; the verb is understood by ellipsis (note the preceding line).
n2305919: tn The plural noun form may indicate degree here.
n2305920: tn Or вАЬwalk aboutвАЭ; NCV вАЬall we have is darkness.вАЭ
n2305921: tn The plural noun form may indicate degree here.
n2305923: tn Heb among the strong, like dead men.вАЭ
n2305925: tn Heb вАЬfor many are our rebellious deeds before you.вАЭ
n2305926: tn Heb вАЬindeed [or вАЬforвАЭ] our rebellious deeds (are) with us, and our sins, we know them.вАЭ
n2305927: tn Heb вАЬspeaking.вАЭ A new sentence was started here in the translation for stylistic reasons.
n2305928: tn Heb вАЬconceiving and uttering from the heart words of falsehood.вАЭ
n2305929: tn Or вАЬrighteousnessвАЭ (ASV, NASB, NIV, NRSV); KJV, NAB вАЬjustice.вАЭ
n2305931: tn Heb вАЬand it is displeasing in his eyes.вАЭ
n2305932: tn Heb вАЬmanвАЭ (so KJV, ASV); TEV вАЬno one to help.вАЭ
n2305933: tn Or вАЬappalledвАЭ (NAB, NIV, NRSV), or вАЬdisgusted.вАЭ
n2305934: tn Heb вАЬand his arm delivers for him.вАЭ
n2305935: tn Heb вАЬand his justice [or вАЬrighteousnessвАЭ] supports him.вАЭ
n2305936: tn Or вАЬrighteousnessвАЭ (KJV, NASB, NIV, NRSV, NLT); NCV вАЬgoodness.вАЭ
n2305937: tn Or вАЬa breastplateвАЭ (traditional; so many English versions); TEV вАЬa coat of armour.вАЭ
n2305938: tn Heb вАЬand [as] a helmet deliverance on his head.вАЭ
n2305939: tn Heb вАЬand he puts on the clothes of vengeance [as] a garment.вАЭ
n2305940: tn Heb вАЬin accordance with deeds, so he repays, anger to his adversaries, repayment to his enemies.вАЭ
n2305942: tc Heb вАЬfear.вАЭ A few medieval Hebrew mss read вАЬsee.вАЭ
n2305943: tn Heb вАЬand they fear from the west the name of the Lord.вАЭ
n2305944: tn Heb вАЬand from the rising of the sun his splendor.вАЭ
n2305946: tn Heb вАЬthe wind of the Lord drives it on.вАЭ The term вАП„®„Х÷Љ„Ч÷ЈвАО (ruakh) could be translated вАЬbreathвАЭ here (see 30:28).
n2305947: tn Or вАЬredeemer.вАЭ See the note at 41:14.
n2305948: tn Heb вАЬand to those who turn from rebellion in Jacob.вАЭ
n2305949:
tn Or вАЬmy covenant withвАЭ (so many English versions); NCV вАЬmy agreement with.вАЭ
sn The Lord promises the repentant (note вАЬto themвАЭ) that they and their offspring will possess his spirit and function as his spokesmen. In this regard they follow in the footsteps of the LordвАЩs special servant. See 42:1; 49:2; 51:16.
n2305950: tn Heb вАЬfrom now and on into the future.вАЭ
n230602: tn The verb вАЬcoversвАЭ is understood by ellipsis (note the preceding line).
n230603: tn Or вАЬgloryвАЭ (so most English versions); TEV вАЬthe brightness of his presence.вАЭ
n230604: tn Heb вАЬLift up around your eyes and see!вАЭ
n230605: tn Or вАЬshine,вАЭ or вАЬbe radiantвАЭ (NAB, NASB, NIV, NRSV).
n230606: tn Heb вАЬand it will tremble and be wide, your heart.вАЭ
n230607: tn Heb вАЬthe wealth of the sea,вАЭ i.e., wealth that is transported from distant lands via the sea.
n230608: tn Heb вАЬan abundance of camels will cover you.вАЭ
n2306010: tn Heb вАЬand they will announce the praises of the Lord.вАЭ
n2306011: tn Heb вАЬwill serve you,вАЭ i.e., be available as sacrifices (see the next line). Another option is to understood these вАЬramsвАЭ as symbolic of leaders who will be subject to the people of Zion. See v. 10.
n2306012: tc Heb вАЬthey will go up on acceptance [on] my altar.вАЭ Some have suggested that the preposition вАП„Ґ÷Ј„ЬвАО ( њal) is dittographic (note the preceding вАП„Щ÷Ј„Ґ÷≤„Ь„Х÷ЉвАО [ya њalu]). Consequently, the form should be emended to вАП„Ь÷∞„®÷Є„¶„Х÷є„ЯвАО (leratson, вАЬacceptablyвАЭ; see BDB 953 s.v. вАП„®÷Є„¶„Х÷є„ЯвАО). However, the Qumran scroll 1QIsaa has both вАП„Ь„®„¶„Х„ЯвАО followed by the preposition вАП„Ґ„ЬвАО, which would argue against deleting the preposition. As the above translation seeks to demonstrate, the preposition вАП„Ґ÷Ј„ЬвАО ( њal) indicates a norm (вАЬin accordance with acceptanceвАЭ or вАЬacceptablyвАЭ; IBHS 218 ¬І11.2.13e, n. 111), and the вАЬaltarвАЭ functions as an objective accusative with a verb of motion (cf. Gen 49:4; Lev 2:2; Num 13:17; J. N. Oswalt, Isaiah [NICOT], 2:534, n. 14).
n2306013: tn Heb вАЬflyвАЭ (so KJV, NASB, NRSV); NAB, NIV вАЬfly along.вАЭ
n2306014: tn Heb вАЬto their windows,вАЭ i.e., to the openings in their coops. See HALOT 83 s.v. вАП„Р÷≤„®÷ї„С÷Љ÷Є„ФвАО.
n2306015: tn Or вАЬislandsвАЭ (NIV); CEV вАЬdistant islandsвАЭ; TEV вАЬdistant lands.вАЭ
n2306016: tn Heb вАЬthe ships of Tarshish.вАЭ See the note at 2:16.
n2306017: tn Heb вАЬto the name of the Lord your God.вАЭ
n2306018: sn See the note on the phrase вАЬthe Holy One of IsraelвАЭ in 1:4.
n2306019: tn Heb вАЬin my favor I will have compassion on you.вАЭ
n2306020: tn Or вАЬled in procession.вАЭ The participle is passive.
n2306021: tn Or вАЬForвАЭ (KJV, NAB, NASB, NIV, NRSV, NLT); TEV вАЬBut.вАЭ
n2306022: tn The infinitive absolute appears before the finite verb for emphasis.
n2306024: tn Heb вАЬthe place of my feet.вАЭ See Ezek 43:7, where the LordвАЩs throne is called the вАЬplace of the soles of my feet.вАЭ
n2306025: sn See the note on the phrase вАЬthe Holy One of IsraelвАЭ in 1:4.
n2306026: tn Heb вАЬInstead of your being abandoned and despised, with no one passing through, I will make you.вАЭ
n2306027: sn The nations and kings are depicted as a mother nursing her children. Restored Zion will be nourished by them as she receives their wealth as tribute.
n2306028: tn Or вАЬredeemer.вАЭ See the note at 41:14.
n2306030: tn The words вАЬI will bring youвАЭ are supplied in the translation; they are understood by ellipsis (see the preceding lines).
n2306031: tn The words вАЬI will bring youвАЭ are supplied in the translation; they are understood by ellipsis (see the first two lines of the verse).
n2306032: tn Or вАЬpeaceвАЭ (KJV and many other English versions).
n2306033: tn The plural indicates degree. The language is ironic; in the past Zion was ruled by oppressive tyrants, but now personified prosperity and vindication will be the only things that will вАЬdominateвАЭ the city.
n2306034: tn The words вАЬsounds ofвАЭ are supplied in the translation for stylistic reasons.
n2306035: tn Heb вАЬand your God for your splendor.вАЭ
n2306036: sn In this verse вАЬsunвАЭ and вАЬmoonвАЭ refer to the LordвАЩs light, which will replace the sun and moon (see v. 19). Light here symbolizes the restoration of divine blessing and prosperity in conjunction with the LordвАЩs presence. See 30:26.
n2306037: tn Heb вАЬdaysвАЭ (so KJV, NAB, NIV, NRSV, NLT).
n2306038: tn Or вАЬrighteousвАЭ (NASB, NIV, NRSV, NLT); NAB вАЬjust.вАЭ
n2306039: tn Heb вАЬa shoot of his planting, the work of my hands, to reveal splendor.вАЭ
n2306041: tn Heb вАЬI, the Lord, in its time, I will quickly do it.вАЭ
n230611: tn Heb вАЬanointed,вАЭ i.e., designated to carry out an assigned task.
n230612: sn The speaker is not identified, but he is distinct from the Lord and from ZionвАЩs suffering people. He possesses the divine spirit, is GodвАЩs spokesman, and is sent to release prisoners from bondage. The evidence suggests he is the LordвАЩs special servant, described earlier in the servant songs (see 42:1-4, 7; 49:2, 9; 50:4; see also 51:16).
n230613: tn Or вАЬsentвАЭ (NAB); NCV вАЬhas appointed me.вАЭ
n230616: tn Heb вАЬto announce the year of the LordвАЩs favor, and the day of our GodвАЩs vengeance.
n230617: tn Heb вАЬoil of joyвАЭ (KJV, ASV); NASB, NIV, NRSV вАЬthe oil of gladness.вАЭ
n230619: tn Heb вАЬa faint spiritвАЭ (so NRSV); KJV, ASV вАЬthe spirit of heavinessвАЭ; NASB вАЬa spirit of fainting.вАЭ
n2306110: tn Rather than referring to the character of the people, вАП„¶÷ґ„У÷ґ„ІвАО (tsedeq) may carry the nuance вАЬvindicationвАЭ here, suggesting that GodвАЩs restored people are a testimony to his justice. See v. 2, which alludes to the fact that God will take vengeance against the enemies of his people. Cf. NAB вАЬoaks of justice.вАЭ
n2306111: tn Heb вАЬa planting of the Lord to reveal splendor.вАЭ
n2306112: tn Heb вАЬand the formerly desolate places they will raise up.вАЭ
n2306113: sn The Lord speaks in vv. 7-8 (and possibly v. 9). It is not clear where the servantвАЩs speech (see vv. 1-3a) ends and the LordвАЩs begins. Perhaps the direct address to the people signals the beginning of the LordвАЩs speech.
n2306115: tn The Hebrew text adds, вАЬit will be said concerning you.вАЭ
n2306116: tn Heb вАЬeatвАЭ (KJV, NAB, NASB); NIV вАЬfeed onвАЭ; NLT вАЬbe fed with.вАЭ
n2306117: tc The form in the Hebrew text probably needs to be emended to вАП„Щ÷і„™÷∞„Р÷Ј„Ю÷Љ÷∞„®„Х÷ЉвАО (yit Њammeru), a Hitpael from вАП„Р÷Є„Ю÷Ј„®вАО ( Њamar), meaning вАЬboast aboutвАЭ (see HALOT 67 s.v. II вАП„Р„Ю„®вАО, HALOT 416 s.v. вАП„Щ„Ю„®вАО, and BDB 56 s.v. вАП„Р÷Є„Ю÷Ј„®вАО).
n2306119: tn Heb вАЬinstead of your shame, a double portion.вАЭ
n2306120: tn Heb вАЬand [instead of] humiliation they will rejoice [over] their portion.вАЭ The term вАП„™÷Ј„Ч÷Ј„™вАО (takhat, вАЬinstead ofвАЭ) is understood by ellipsis (note the preceding line).
n2306121: tn Heb вАЬthereforeвАЭ (so KJV, NASB); NIV вАЬand so.вАЭ
n2306122: tn Heb вАЬin faithfulnessвАЭ; NASB, NRSV, NLT вАЬfaithfully.вАЭ
n2306123: tn Heb вАЬall who see them will recognize them, that they [are] descendants [whom] the Lord has blessed.вАЭ
n2306124: sn The speaker in vv. 10-11 is not identified, but it is likely that the personified nation (or perhaps Zion) responds here to the LordвАЩs promise of restoration.
n2306125: tn The infinitive absolute appears before the finite verb for emphasis.
n2306126: tn Heb вАЬmy being is happy in my GodвАЭ; NAB вАЬin my God is the joy of my soul.вАЭ
n2306127: tn Heb вАЬrobe of vindicationвАЭ; KJV, NASB, NIV, NRSV вАЬrobe of righteousness.вАЭ
n2306128: tn Heb вАЬlike a bridegroom [who] acts like a priest [by wearing] a turban, and like a bride [who] wears her jewelry.вАЭ The words вАЬI lookвАЭ are supplied for stylistic reasons and clarification.
n2306129: tn Or perhaps, вАЬrighteousness,вАЭ but the context seems to emphasize deliverance and restoration (see v. 10 and 62:1).
n2306130: tn Heb вАЬand praise before all the nations.вАЭ
n230623: tn Heb вАЬwhich the mouth of the Lord will designate.вАЭ
n230624: tn Or вАЬforвАЭ; KJV, NAB, NASB, NIV, NRSV вАЬbut.вАЭ
n230625: tn Hebrew вАП„Ч÷ґ„§÷∞„¶÷і„Щ÷Њ„С÷Є„Ф÷ЉвАО (kheftsi vah), traditionally transliterated вАЬHephzibahвАЭ (so KJV, ASV, NIV).
n230626: tn Hebrew вАП„С÷Љ÷∞„Ґ„Х÷Љ„Ь÷Є„ФвАО (be њulah), traditionally transliterated вАЬBeulahвАЭ (so KJV, ASV, NIV).
n230627: tn That is, the land will be restored to the LordвАЩs favor and once again enjoy his blessing and protection. To indicate the landвАЩs relationship to the Lord, the words вАЬto himвАЭ have been supplied at the end of the clause.
n230628: tc The Hebrew text has вАЬyour sons,вАЭ but this produces an odd metaphor and is somewhat incongruous with the parallelism. In the context (v. 4b, see also 54:5-7) the Lord is the one who вАЬmarriesвАЭ Zion. Therefore several prefer to emend вАЬyour sonsвАЭ to вАП„С÷Љ÷є„†÷Є„Щ÷і„Ъ÷∞вАО (bonayikh, вАЬyour builderвАЭ; e.g., NRSV). In Ps 147:2 the Lord is called the вАЬbuilder of Jerusalem.вАЭ However, this emendation is not the best option for at least four reasons. First, although the Lord is never called the вАЬbuilderвАЭ of Jerusalem in Isaiah, the idea of ZionвАЩs children possessing the land does occur (Isa 49:20; 54:3; cf. also 14:1; 60:21). Secondly, all the ancient versions support the MT reading. Thirdly, although the verb вАП„С÷Љ÷Є„Ґ÷Ј„ЬвАО (ba њal) can mean вАЬto marry,вАЭ its basic idea is вАЬto possess.вАЭ Consequently, the verb stresses a relationship more than a state. All the ancient versions render this verb вАЬto dwell inвАЭ or вАЬto dwell with.вАЭ The point is not just that the land will be reinhabited, but that it will be in a relationship of вАЬbelongingвАЭ to the Israelites. Hence a relational verb like вАП„С÷Љ÷Є„Ґ÷Ј„ЬвАО is used (J. N. Oswalt, Isaiah [NICOT], 2:581). Finally, вАЬsonsвАЭ is a well-known metaphor for вАЬinhabitantsвАЭ (J. de Waard, Isaiah, 208).
n230629: sn The speaker here is probably the prophet.
n2306210: tn Heb вАЬall day and all night continually they do not keep silent.вАЭ The following lines suggest that they pray for the LordвАЩs intervention and restoration of the city.
n2306211: tn Or вАЬinvokeвАЭ; NIV вАЬcall onвАЭ; NASB, NRSV вАЬremind.вАЭ
n2306212: tn вАЬJerusalemвАЭ is supplied in the translation for stylistic reasons; note the following line.
n2306214: tn The LordвАЩs right hand and strong arm here symbolize his power and remind the audience that his might guarantees the fulfillment of the following promise.
n2306215: tn Heb вАЬit,вАЭ the grain mentioned in v. 8a.
n2306216: tn Heb вАЬand those who gather it will drink it.вАЭ The masculine singular pronominal suffixes attached to вАЬgatherвАЭ and вАЬdrinkвАЭ refer back to the masculine noun вАП„™÷Љ÷і„Щ„®„Х÷є„©„БвАО (tirosh, вАЬwineвАЭ) in v. 8b.
n2306217: tn Heb вАЬto the end of the earthвАЭ (so NASB, NRSV).
n2306218: sn As v. 12 indicates, the returning exiles are the LordвАЩs reward/prize. See also 40:10 and the note there.
n2306219: tn Or вАЬthe redeemed of the LordвАЭ (KJV, NAB).
n230631: sn Edom is here an archetype for the LordвАЩs enemies. See 34:5.
n230632: tn Heb вАЬ[in] bright red garments, from Bozrah.вАЭ
n230633: tn The interrogative particle is understood by ellipsis; note the first line of the verse.
n230634: tn Heb вАЬhonored in his clothingвАЭ; KJV, ASV вАЬglorious in his apparel.вАЭ
n230635: tc The Hebrew text has вАП„¶÷Є„Ґ÷Є„ФвАО (tsa њah), which means вАЬstoop, bendвАЭ (51:14). The translation assumes an emendation to вАП„¶÷Є„Ґ÷Ј„УвАО (tsa њad, вАЬmarchвАЭ; see BDB 858 s.v. вАП„¶÷Є„Ґ÷Є„ФвАО).
n230636: tn Heb вАЬI, [the one] speaking in vindication [or вАЬrighteousnessвАЭ], great to deliver.вАЭ
n230637: tn Heb вАЬand your garments like one who treads in a vat?вАЭ
n230638: sn Nations, headed by Edom, are the object of the LordвАЩs anger (see v. 6). He compares military slaughter to stomping on grapes in a vat.
n230639: tn Heb вАЬand I stained.вАЭ For discussion of the difficult verb form, see HALOT 170 s.v. II вАП„Т„Р„ЬвАО. Perhaps the form is mixed, combining the first person forms of the imperfect (note the alef prefix) and perfect (note the вАП„™„ЩвАО- ending).
n2306310: tn Heb вАЬfor the day of vengeance was in my heart, and the year of my revenge came.вАЭ The term вАП„Т÷Љ÷∞„Р„Х÷Љ„Ь÷Ј„ЩвАО (ge Њulay) is sometimes translated here вАЬmy redemption,вАЭ for the verbal root вАП„Т„Р„ЬвАО often means вАЬdeliver, buy back.вАЭ A вАП„Т÷Љ÷є„Р÷µ„ЬвАО (go Њel, вАЬkinsman-redeemerвАЭ) was responsible for protecting the extended familyвАЩs interests, often by redeeming property that had been sold outside the family. However, the responsibilities of a вАП„Т÷Љ÷є„Р÷µ„ЬвАО extended beyond financial concerns. He was also responsible for avenging the shed blood of a family member (see Num 35:19-27; Deut 19:6-12). In Isa 63:4, where vengeance is a prominent theme (note the previous line), it is probably this function of the family protector that is in view. The Lord pictures himself as a blood avenger who waits for the day of vengeance to arrive and then springs into action.
n2306312: tn Heb вАЬand my anger, it supported meвАЭ; NIV вАЬmy own wrath sustained me.вАЭ
n2306313: sn See Isa 49:26 and 51:23 for similar imagery.
n2306314: tn Heb вАЬand I brought down to the ground their juice.вАЭ вАЬJuiceвАЭ refers to their blood (see v. 3).
n2306316: tn Heb вАЬgreatness of goodness to the house of Israel which he did for them.вАЭ
n2306318: tn Heb вАЬchildren [who] do not act deceitfully.вАЭ Here the verb refers to covenantal loyalty.
n2306319: tn Heb вАЬin all their distress, there was distress to himвАЭ (reading вАП„Ь„Х÷євАО [lo] with the margin/Qere).
n2306320:
tn Heb вАЬthe messenger [or вАЬangelвАЭ] of his faceвАЭ; NIV вАЬthe angel of his presence.вАЭ
sn This may refer to the вАЬangel of GodвАЭ mentioned in Exod 14:19, who in turn may be identical to the divine вАЬpresenceвАЭ (literally, вАЬfaceвАЭ) referred to in Exod 33:14-15 and Deut 4:37. Here in Isa 63 this messenger may be equated with GodвАЩs вАЬholy SpiritвАЭ (see vv. 10-11) and вАЬthe Spirit of the LordвАЭ (v. 14). See also Ps 139:7, where GodвАЩs вАЬSpiritвАЭ seems to be equated with his вАЬpresenceвАЭ (literally, вАЬfaceвАЭ) in the synonymous parallelistic structure.
n2306321: tn Or вАЬredeemedвАЭ (KJV, NAB, NIV), or вАЬdelivered.вАЭ
n2306322: tn Heb вАЬall the days of antiquityвАЭ; KJV, NAB, NASB, NIV, NRSV вАЬdays of old.вАЭ
n2306323: tn Or вАЬgrieved, hurt the feelings of.вАЭ
n2306324: sn The phrase вАЬholy SpiritвАЭ occurs in the OT only here (in v. 11 as well) and in Ps 51:11 (51:13 HT), where it is associated with the divine presence.
n2306325: tn Heb вАЬand he remembered the days of antiquity, Moses, his people.вАЭ The syntax of the statement is unclear. The translation assumes that вАЬhis peopleвАЭ is the subject of the verb вАЬremembered.вАЭ If original, вАЬMosesвАЭ is in apposition to вАЬthe days of antiquity,вАЭ more precisely identifying the time period referred to. However, the syntactical awkwardness suggests that вАЬMosesвАЭ may have been an early marginal note (perhaps identifying вАЬthe shepherd of his flockвАЭ two lines later) that has worked its way into the text.
n2306326: tn The Hebrew text has a plural form, which if retained and taken as a numerical plural, would probably refer to Moses, Aaron, and the Israelite tribal leaders at the time of the Exodus. Most prefer to emend the form to the singular (вАП„®÷Є„Ґ÷Є„ФвАО, ra њah) and understand this as a reference just to Moses.
n2306328: tn Heb вАЬwho caused to go at the right hand of Moses the arm of his splendor.вАЭ
n2306329: tn Heb вАЬmaking for himself a lasting name.вАЭ
n2306331: tn The words вАЬto grazeвАЭ are supplied in the translation for clarification.
n2306332: tn Or вАЬsoвАЭ (KJV, ASV), or вАЬthusвАЭ (NAB, NRSV).
n2306333: tn Heb вАЬmaking for yourself a majestic name.вАЭ
n2306334: tn This probably refers to his zeal for his people, which motivates him to angrily strike out against their enemies.
n2306335: tn The Hebrew text reads literally, вАЬthe agitation of your intestines and your compassion to me they are held back.вАЭ The phrase вАЬagitation of your intestinesвАЭ is metonymic, referring to the way in which oneвАЩs nervous system reacts when one feels pity and compassion toward another. вАП„Р÷µ„Ь÷Ј„ЩвАО ( Њelay, вАЬto meвАЭ) is awkward in this context, where the speaker represents the nation and, following the introduction (see v. 7), utilizes first person plural forms. The translation assumes an emendation to the negative particle вАП„Р÷Ј„ЬвАО ( Њal). This also necessitates emending the following verb form (which is a plural perfect) to a singular jussive (вАП„™÷і„™÷∞„Р÷Ј„§÷Љ÷Є„ІвАО, tit Њappaq). The Hitpael of вАП„Р÷Є„§÷Ј„ІвАО ( Њafaq) also occurs in 42:14.
n2306336: tn Heb вАЬour protector [or вАЬredeemerвАЭ] from antiquity [is] your name.вАЭ
n2306337: tn Some suggest a tolerative use of the Hiphil here, вАЬ[why do] you allow us to stray?вАЭ (cf. NLT). Though the Hiphil of вАП„™÷Є„Ґ÷Є„ФвАО (ta њah) appears to be tolerative in Jer 50:6, elsewhere it is preferable or necessary to take it as causative. See Isa 3:12; 9:15; and 30:28, as well as Gen 20:13; 2 Kgs 21:9; Job 12:24-25; Prov 12:26; Jer 23:13, 32; Hos 4:12; Amos 2:4; Mic 3:5.
n2306338: tn This probably refers to GodвАЩs commands.
n2306339:
tn Heb вАЬ[Why do] you harden our heart[s] so as not to fear you.вАЭ The interrogative particle is understood by ellipsis (note the preceding line).
sn How direct this hardening is, one cannot be sure. The speaker may envision direct involvement on the LordвАЩs part. The Lord has brought the exile as judgment for the nationвАЩs sin and now he continues to keep them at armвАЩs length by blinding them spiritually. The second half of 64:7 might support this, though the precise reading of the final verb is uncertain. On the other hand, the idiom of lament is sometimes ironic and hyperbolically deterministic. For example, Naomi lamented that Shaddai was directly opposing her and bringing her calamity (Ruth 1:20-21), while the author of Ps 88 directly attributes his horrible suffering and loneliness to God (see especially vv. 6-8, 16-18). Both individuals make little, if any, room for intermediate causes or the principle of sin and death which ravages the human race. In the same way, the speaker in Isa 63:17 (who evidences great spiritual sensitivity and is anything but вАЬhardenedвАЭ) may be referring to the hardships of exile, which discouraged and even embittered the people, causing many of them to retreat from their Yahwistic faith. In this case, the вАЬhardeningвАЭ in view is more indirect and can be lifted by the LordвАЩs intervention. Whether the hardening here is indirect or direct, it is important to recognize that the speaker sees it as one of the effects of rebellion against the Lord (note especially 64:5-6).
n2306340: tn Or вАЬholyвАЭ (ASV, NASB, NRSV, TEV, NLT).
n2306341: tn Heb вАЬfor a short time they had a possession, the people of your holiness.вАЭ
n2306343: tn Heb вАЬwe were from antiquityвАЭ (see v. 16). The collocation of вАП„Ф÷Є„Щ÷Є„ФвАО, вАП„Ю÷і„ЯвАО, and вАП„Ґ„Х÷є„Ь÷Є„ЭвАО (hayah, min, and  њolam) occurs only here.
n2306344: tn Heb вАЬyou did not rule them; your name was not called over them.вАЭ The expression вАЬthe name is called overвАЭ indicates ownership; see the note at 4:1. As these two lines stand, they are very difficult to interpret. They appear to be stating that the adversaries just mentioned in v. 18 have not been subject to the LordвАЩs rule in the past, perhaps explaining why they could commit the atrocity described in v. 18b.
n230641: sn In BHS the chapter division occurs in a different place from the English Bible: 64:1 ET (63:19b HT) and 64:2-12 (64:1-11 HT). Beginning with 65:1 the verse numbers in the English Bible and the Hebrew Bible are again the same.
n230642: tn Or вАЬthe heavens.вАЭ The Hebrew term вАП„©÷Є„Б„Ю÷Ј„Щ÷і„ЭвАО (shamayim) may be translated вАЬheavensвАЭ or вАЬskyвАЭ depending on the context.
n230643: tn Or вАЬquake.вАЭ вАП„†÷Є„Ц÷є„Ь÷Љ„Х÷ЉвАО (nazollu) is from the verbal root вАП„Ц÷Є„Ь÷Ј„ЬвАО (zalal, вАЬquakeвАЭ; see HALOT 272 s.v. II вАП„Ц„Ь„ЬвАО). Perhaps there is a verbal allusion to Judg 5:5, the only other passage where this verb occurs. In that passage the poet tells how the LordвАЩs appearance to do battle caused the mountains to shake.
n230644: tn Heb вАЬto make known your name to your adversaries.вАЭ Perhaps the infinitive construct with preposition вАП„Ь÷∞вАО (lamed) should be construed with вАЬcome downвАЭ in v. 1a, or subordinated to the following line: вАЬTo make known your name to your adversaries, let the nations shake from before you.вАЭ
n230645: tn Heb вАЬ[for which] we were not waiting.вАЭ
n230647: tn Heb вАЬfrom ancient times they have not heard, they have not listened.вАЭ
n230649: tn Heb вАЬthe one who rejoices and does righteousness.вАЭ
n2306410: tn Heb вАЬin your ways they remember you.вАЭ
n2306411: tc The Hebrew text reads literally, вАЬlook, you were angry, and we sinned against them continually [or perhaps, вАЬin ancient timesвАЭ] and we were delivered.вАЭ The statement makes little sense as it stands. The first vav [вАП„ХвАО] consecutive (вАЬand we sinnedвАЭ) must introduce an explanatory clause here (see Num 1:48 and Isa 39:1 for other examples of this relatively rare use of the vav [вАП„ХвАО] consecutive). The final verb (if rendered positively) makes no sense in this contextвАФGodвАЩs anger at their sin resulted in judgment, not deliverance. One of the alternatives involves an emendation to вАП„Х÷Ј„†÷Љ÷і„®÷∞„©„Б÷Є„ҐвАО (vannirsha њ, вАЬand we were evilвАЭ; LXX, NRSV, TEV). The Vulgate and the Qumran scroll 1QIsaa support the MT reading. One can either accept an emendation or cast the statement as a question (as above).
n2306412: tn Heb вАЬand like a garment of menstruation [are] all our righteous actsвАЭ; KJV, NIV вАЬfilthy ragsвАЭ; ASV вАЬa polluted garment.вАЭ
n2306413: tn Or вАЬcalls out inвАЭ; NASB, NIV, NRSV вАЬcalls on.вАЭ
n2306414: tn Or вАЬrouses himselfвАЭ; NASB вАЬarouses himself.вАЭ
n2306415: tn Heb вАЬfor you have hidden your face from us.вАЭ
n2306416: tc The Hebrew text reads literally, вАЬand you caused us to melt in the hand of our sin.вАЭ The verb вАП„Х÷Ј„™÷Љ÷∞„Ю„Х÷Љ„Т÷µ„†„Х÷ЉвАО (vattemugenu) is a Qal preterite second person masculine singular with a first person common plural suffix from the root вАП„Ю„Х÷Љ„ТвАО (mug, вАЬmeltвАЭ). However, elsewhere the Qal of this verb is intransitive. If the verbal root вАП„Ю„Х÷Љ„ТвАО (mug) is retained here, the form should be emended to a Polel pattern (вАП„Х÷Ј„™÷Љ÷∞„Ю÷є„Т÷∞„Т÷µ„†„Х÷ЉвАО, vattemogegenu). The translation assumes an emendation to вАП„Х÷Ј„™÷Љ÷∞„Ю÷Ј„Т÷Љ÷∞„†÷µ„†„Х÷ЉвАО (vattemaggenenu, вАЬand you handed us overвАЭ). This form is a Piel preterite second person masculine singular with a first person common plural suffix from the verb вАП„Ю÷Є„Т÷Є„ЯвАО (magan, вАЬhand over, surrenderвАЭ; see HALOT 545 s.v. вАП„Ю„Т„ЯвАО and BDB 171 s.v. вАП„Ю÷Є„Т÷Є„ЯвАО). The point is that God has abandoned them to their sinful ways and no longer seeks reconciliation.
n2306417: tn On the force of вАП„Х÷∞„Ґ÷Ј„™÷Љ÷Є„ФвАО (ve њattah) here, see HALOT 902 s.v. вАП„Ґ÷Ј„™÷Љ÷Є„ФвАО.
n2306420: tn Heb вАЬLook, gaze at your people, all of us.вАЭ Another option is to translate, вАЬTake a good look! We are all your people.вАЭ
n2306421: tn Heb вАЬholyвАЭ (so KJV, NASB, NRSV, NLT); NIV вАЬsacred.вАЭ
n2306424: tn Or вАЬall that we valued has become a ruin.вАЭ
n2306425: tn Heb вАЬbecause of theseвАЭ; KJV, ASV вАЬfor these things.вАЭ
n230651: tn Heb вАЬI allowed myself to be sought by those who did not ask.вАЭ
n230652: tn Heb вАЬI allowed myself to be found by those who did not seek.вАЭ
n230653: tn Heb вАЬcall out inвАЭ; NASB, NIV, NRSV вАЬcall on.вАЭ
n230654: tn Heb вАЬwho walked [in] the way that is not good, after their thoughts.вАЭ
n230655: tn Heb вАЬthe people who provoke me to anger to my face continually.вАЭ
n230656: tn Or вАЬgardensвАЭ (KJV, NASB, NIV, NRSV, NLT).
n230658: sn Perhaps the worship of underworld deities or dead spirits is in view.
n230659: tn The Hebrew text reads literally, вАЬand in the watches they spend the night.вАЭ Some understand вАП„†÷Љ÷∞„¶„Х÷Љ„®÷і„Щ„ЭвАО (netsurim) as referring to вАЬsecret placesвАЭ or вАЬcaves,вАЭ while others emend the text to вАП„Х÷Љ„С÷µ„Щ„Я „¶„Х÷Љ„®÷і„Щ„ЭвАО (uven tsurim, вАЬbetween the rocky cliffsвАЭ).
n2306510: tn Heb вАЬthe flesh of the pigвАЭ; KJV, NAB, NASB вАЬswineвАЩs flesh.вАЭ
n2306511: tc The marginal reading (Qere), supported by the Qumran scroll 1QIsaa, reads вАП„Ю÷∞„®÷Ј„ІвАО (meraq, вАЬbrothвАЭ), while the consonantal text (Kethib) has вАП„§÷∞„®÷Ј„ІвАО (feraq, вАЬfragmentвАЭ).
n2306512: tn Heb вАЬLook, it is written before me.вАЭ
n2306513: tn Heb вАЬI will pay back into their lap.вАЭ
n2306515: tn Or perhaps, вАЬtauntedвАЭ; KJV вАЬblasphemedвАЭ; NAB вАЬdisgracedвАЭ; NASB вАЬscornedвАЭ; NIV вАЬdefiedвАЭ; NRSV вАЬreviled.вАЭ
n2306516: tn Heb вАЬI will measure out their pay [from the] beginning into their lap,вАЭ i.e., he will give them everything they have earned.
n2306517: tn Heb вАЬjust as.вАЭ In the Hebrew text the statement is one long sentence, вАЬJust asвА¶, so I will doвА¶.вАЭ
n2306518: tn Heb вАЬfor a blessing is in it.вАЭ
n2306520: tn Heb вАЬit.вАЭ The third feminine singular pronominal suffix probably refers to the land that contains the aforementioned mountains.
n2306521: sn Sharon was a plain located to the west, along the Mediterranean coast north of Joppa and south of Carmel.
n2306522: sn The Valley of Achor (вАЬAchorвАЭ means вАЬtroubleвАЭ in Hebrew) was the site of AchanвАЩs execution. It was located to the east, near Jericho.
n2306523: tn Heb вАЬa resting place for cattleвАЭ; NASB, NIV вАЬfor herds.вАЭ
n2306524: tn Heb вАЬfor my people who seek me.вАЭ
n2306525: tn The Hebrew text has simply, вАЬforget.вАЭ The words вАЬabout worshiping atвАЭ are supplied in the translation for clarification.
n2306526: tn The Hebrew has вАП„Ь÷Ј„Т÷Љ÷Ј„УвАО (laggad, вАЬfor GadвАЭ), the name of a pagan deity. See HALOT 176 s.v. II вАП„Т÷Љ÷Ј„УвАО 2.
n2306527: tn The Hebrew has вАП„Ь÷Ј„Ю÷∞„†÷і„ЩвАО (lamni, вАЬfor MeniвАЭ), the name of a pagan deity. See HALOT 602 s.v. вАП„Ю÷∞„Т÷і„ЩвАО.
n2306528: tn Heb вАЬI assign you to the sword.вАЭ Some emend the Qal verb form вАП„Ю÷Є„†÷і„Щ„™÷і„ЩвАО (maniti, вАЬI assignвАЭ) to the Piel вАП„Ю÷і„†÷Љ÷і„Щ„™÷і„ЩвАО (minniti, вАЬ I ordainвАЭ). The verb sounds like the name of the god Meni (вАП„Ю÷∞„†÷і„ЩвАО, meni, вАЬDestiny, FateвАЭ). The sound play draws attention to the irony of the statement. The sinners among GodвАЩs people worship the god Meni, apparently in an effort to ensure a bright destiny for themselves. But the Lord is the one who really determines their destiny and he has decreed their demise.
n2306529: tn Or вАЬat the slaughterвАЭ; NIV вАЬfor the slaughterвАЭ; NLT вАЬbefore the executioner.вАЭ
n2306530: tn Heb вАЬthat which is evil in my eyes.вАЭ
n2306531: tn Heb вАЬfrom the good of the heart.вАЭ
n2306532: tn Heb вАЬfrom the pain of the heart.вАЭ
n2306533: tn Heb вАЬfrom the breaking of the spirit.вАЭ
n2306534:
tn Heb вАЬyou will leave your name for an oath to my chosen ones.вАЭ

n2306535: tn Or вАЬin the landвАЭ (NIV, NCV, NRSV). The same phrase occurs again later in this verse, with the same options.
n2306536: tn Heb вАЬwill pronounce a blessing by the God of truth.вАЭ
n2306537: tn Heb вАЬwill take an oath by the God of truth.вАЭ
n2306538: tn Heb вАЬfor the former distresses will be forgotten, and they will be hidden from my eyes.вАЭ
n2306539: sn This hyperbolic statement likens the coming transformation of Jerusalem (see vv. 18-19) to a new creation of the cosmos.
n2306540: tn Or perhaps, вАЬthe former thingsвАЭ (so ASV, NASB, NIV, NRSV); TEV вАЬThe events of the past.вАЭ
n2306541: tn Heb вАЬand they will not come up on the mind.вАЭ
n2306543: tn Heb вАЬJerusalem, joy.вАЭ The next verse suggests the meaning: The Lord will create Jerusalem to be a source of joy to himself.
n2306544: tn Heb вАЬher people, happiness.вАЭ See the preceding note.
n2306545: tn Heb вАЬand I will rejoice in Jerusalem and be happy in my people.вАЭ
n2306546: tn Heb вАЬand there will not be from there again a nursing infant of days,вАЭ i.e., one that lives just a few days.
n2306547: tn Heb вАЬor an old [man] who does not fill out his days.вАЭ
n2306548: tn Heb вАЬfor the child as a son of one hundred years will die.вАЭ The point seems to be that those who die at the age of a hundred will be considered children, for the average life span will be much longer than that. The category вАЬchildвАЭ will be redefined in light of the expanded life spans that will characterize this new era.
n2306549: tn Heb вАЬthe one who misses.вАЭ вАП„Ч÷Є„Ш÷Є„РвАО (khata Њ) is used here in its basic sense of вАЬmiss the mark.вАЭ See HALOT 305 s.v. вАП„Ч„Ш„РвАО. Another option is to translate, вАЬand the sinner who reaches the age of a hundred will be cursed.вАЭ
n2306550: tn Heb вАЬthey will not build, and another live [in it].вАЭ
n2306551: tn Heb вАЬthey will not plant, and another eat.вАЭ
n2306552: tn Heb вАЬfor like the days of the tree [will be] the days of my people.вАЭ
n2306553: tn Heb вАЬthe work of their handsвАЭ (so KJV, NASB, NIV, NRSV); NLT вАЬtheir hard-won gains.вАЭ
n2306554: tn Heb вАЬand they will not give birth to horror.вАЭ
n2306555: tn Heb вАЬfor offspring blessed by the Lord they [will be], and their descendants along with them.вАЭ
n2306556: tn The verb that introduces this verse serves as a discourse particle and is untranslated; see note on вАЬin the futureвАЭ in 2:2.
n2306559: sn Some see an allusion to Gen 3:14 (note вАЬyou will eat dirtвАЭ). The point would be that even in this new era the snake (often taken as a symbol of Satan) remains under GodвАЩs curse. However, it is unlikely that such an allusion exists. Even if there is an echo of Gen 3:14, the primary allusion is to 11:8, where snakes are pictured as no longer dangerous. They will no longer attack other living creatures, but will be content to crawl along the ground. (The statement вАЬyou will eat dirtвАЭ in Gen 3:14 means вАЬyou will crawl on the ground.вАЭ In the same way the statement вАЬdirt will be its foodвАЭ in Isa 65:25 means вАЬit will crawl on the ground.вАЭ)
n2306560:
tn Heb вАЬin all my holy mountain.вАЭ These same words appear in 11:9. See the note there.
sn As in 11:1-9 the prophet anticipates a time when the categories predator-prey no longer exist. See the note at the end of 11:8.
n230661: tn Heb вАЬall these.вАЭ The phrase refers to the heavens and earth, mentioned in the previous verse.
n230662: tn Heb вАЬand all these were.вАЭ Some prefer to emend вАП„Х÷Ј„Щ÷Љ÷і„Ф÷∞„Щ„Х÷ЉвАО (vayyihyu, вАЬand they wereвАЭ) to вАП„Х÷∞„Ь÷і„Щ „Ф÷Є„Щ„Х÷ЉвАО (veli hayu, вАЬand to me they wereвАЭ), i.e., вАЬand they belong to me.вАЭ
n230663: tn Heb вАЬand to this one I lookвАЭ (KJV and NASB both similar).
n230664: tn Heb вАЬto the humble and the lowly in spirit and the one who trembles at my words.вАЭ
n230665: tn Heb вАЬone who slaughters a bull, one who strikes down a man.вАЭ Some understand a comparison here and in the following lines. In GodвАЩs sight the one who sacrifices is like (i.e., regarded as) a murderer or one whose worship is ritually defiled or idolatrous. The translation above assumes that the language is not metaphorical, but descriptive of the sinnersвАЩ hypocritical behavior. (Note the last two lines of the verse, which suggest they are guilty of abominable practices.) On the one hand, they act pious and offer sacrifices, but at the same time they commit violent crimes against men, defile their sacrifices, and worship other gods.
n230666:
tn Heb вАЬone who sacrifices a lamb, one who breaks a dogвАЩs neck.вАЭ Some understand a comparison, but see the previous note.
sn The significance of breaking a dogвАЩs neck is uncertain, though the structure of the statement when compared to the preceding and following lines suggests the action is viewed in a negative light. According to Exod 13:13 and 34:20, one was to вАЬredeemвАЭ a firstborn donkey by offering a lamb; if one did not вАЬredeemвАЭ the firstborn donkey in this way, then its neck must be broken. According to Deut 21:1-9 a heiferвАЩs neck was to be broken as part of the atonement ritual to purify the land from the guilt of bloodshed. It is not certain if these passages relate in any way to the action described in Isa 66:3.
n230667: tn Heb вАЬone who offers an offering, pigвАЩs blood.вАЭ Some understand a comparison, but see the note at the end of the first line.
n230668: tn Heb вАЬone who offers incense as a memorial offering, one who blesses something false.вАЭ Some understand a comparison, but see the note at the end of the first line. вАП„Р÷Є„Х÷ґ„ЯвАО ( Њaven), which has a wide variety of attested nuances, here refers metonymically to an idol. See HALOT 22 s.v. and BDB 20 s.v. 2.
n230669: tn Heb вАЬalso they have chosen their ways.вАЭ
n2306610: tn Heb вАЬtheir being [or вАЬsoulвАЭ] takes delight in their disgusting [things].вАЭ
n2306611: tn The precise meaning of the noun is uncertain. It occurs only here and in 3:4 (but see the note there). It appears to be derived from the verbal root вАП„Ґ÷Є„Ь÷Ј„ЬвАО ( њalal), which can carry the nuance вАЬdeal severely.вАЭ
n2306612: tn Heb вАЬthat which is evil in my eyes.вАЭ
n2306614: tn Heb вАЬbrothersвАЭ (so NASB, NIV); NRSV вАЬYour own peopleвАЭ; NLT вАЬYour close relatives.вАЭ

n2306616: tn Heb вАЬland,вАЭ but here вАП„Р÷ґ„®÷ґ„•вАО ( Њerets) stands metonymically for an organized nation (see the following line).

n2306618: tn Or вАЬin order thatвАЭ; ASV, NRSV вАЬthat.вАЭ

n2306631: tn Heb вАЬthe ones who consecrate themselves and the ones who purify themselves toward the orchards [or вАЬgardensвАЭ] after the one in the midst.вАЭ The precise meaning of the statement is unclear, though it is obvious that some form of idolatry is in view.
n2306632: tn Heb вАЬones who eat the flesh of the pig and the disgusting thing and the mouse.вАЭ
n2306633: tn Heb вАЬtogether they will come to an end.вАЭ
n2306634: tc The Hebrew text reads literally вАЬand I, their deeds and their thoughts, am coming.вАЭ The syntax here is very problematic, suggesting that the text may need emendation. Some suggest that the words вАЬtheir deeds and their thoughtsвАЭ have been displaced from v. 17. This line presents two primary challenges. In the first place, the personal pronoun вАЬIвАЭ has no verb after it. Most translations insert вАЬknowвАЭ for the sake of clarity (NASB, NRSV, NLT, ESV). The NIV has вАЬI, because of their actions and their imaginationsвА¶вАЭ Since GodвАЩs вАЬknowledgeвАЭ of IsraelвАЩs sin occasions judgment, the verb вАЬhateвАЭ is an option as well (see above translation). The feminine form of the next verb (вАП„С÷Љ÷Є„Р÷Є„ФвАО, ba Њah) could be understood in one of two ways. One could provide an implied noun вАЬtimeвАЭ (вАП„Ґ÷µ„™вАО,  њet) and render the next line вАЬthe time is coming/has comeвАЭ (NASB, ESV). One could also emend the feminine verb to the masculine вАП„С÷Љ÷Є„РвАО (ba Њ) and have the вАЬIвАЭ at the beginning of the line govern this verb as well (for the Lord is speaking here): вАЬI am comingвАЭ (cf. NIV, NCV, NRSV, TEV, NLT).
